frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment: Appa did the right thing 100%. These girls don't understand what kind of danger they are putting themselves into by running away with some random guy — father and brothers are there to protect, not to control. If he had not stopped her that day, tomorrow she would be crying somewhere abandoned and the whole family name also gone. Daughters have a responsibility to their family before they think about their own 'freedom'. #FamilyFirst #RespectYourParents
label: misogynistic or non_misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment frames the father's forcible intervention as protective rather than controlling, denying the woman's right to autonomous decision-making and consent. It encodes paternalistic ownership by positioning male family members as legitimate arbiters of a woman's choices, and frames her agency as a threat to family honour rather than a basic right.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
